title: Chuck Hagel on Office Hours
summary: Chuck Hagel, 24th U.S. Secretary of Defense, former U.S. Senator from Nebraska, and former Belfer Center Visiting Fellow, talks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about his advice to the next Secretary of State, bringing Bowe Bergdahl home, being a pioneer in the cellphone industry, and how he likes to insert a bit of fun into a serious job.
body: Chuck Hagel, 24th U.S. Secretary of Defense, former U.S. Senator from Nebraska, and former Belfer Center Visiting Fellow, talks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about his advice to the next Secretary of State, bringing Bowe Bergdahl home, being a pioneer in the cellphone industry, and how he likes to insert a bit of fun into a serious job. The latest from Belfer See more Gallery 1 of 4 Chuck Hagel: Advice from one SecDef to Another 2 of 4 Chuck Hagel: The Bowe Bergdahl Swap 3 of 4 Chuck Hagel: When Chuck Hagel Wore A Joe Biden Costume 4 of 4 Chuck Hagel: Cellphone Pioneer Chuck Hagel was the 24th Secretary of Defense, serving from February 2013 to February 2015. He is the only Vietnam veteran and the first enlisted combat veteran to serve as Secretary of Defense. Secretary Hagel also served two terms in the United States Senate (1997-2009) representing the state of Nebraska. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: In this interview, former U.S. Secretary of Defense Chuck Hagel discusses a range of topics from his career and policy perspectives. He offers advice to the next Secretary of State, reflecting on the demands of high-level diplomatic and security roles. Hagel addresses the controversial decision to secure the release of U.S. Army soldier Bowe Bergdahl, a topic of significant debate. He also shares personal anecdotes, including his early involvement in the cellphone industry as a pioneer and his approach to incorporating humor into serious governmental work, such as wearing a Joe Biden costume. The conversation provides insights into his unique background as the only Vietnam veteran and first enlisted combat veteran to serve as Secretary of Defense, as well as his tenure as a U.S. Senator from Nebraska.
result.main_content_zh: 在这篇访谈中，美国前国防部长查克·哈格尔讨论了其职业生涯和政策观点的一系列话题。他为下一任国务卿提供建议，反思了高层外交与安全职务的要求。哈格尔谈及了确保美国陆军士兵鲍伊·伯格达尔获释这一颇具争议的决定，该话题曾引发重大辩论。他还分享了一些个人轶事，包括他作为先驱早期参与手机行业的经历，以及他如何在严肃的政府工作中融入幽默感，例如曾穿戴乔·拜登的服装。对话揭示了他作为唯一一位曾担任国防部长的越战老兵、首位担任该职的入伍战斗老兵，以及内布拉斯加州前美国参议员的独特背景与任期经历。
